briefdescription.para: Baseadress of DDR region reserved for PLM.
Size of DDR region reserved for PLM.
MJTAG word aligner enable status check mask.
RTCA Register to save IPI Mask.
RTCA Register to save Response Buffer Address.
RTCA Register to store timeout in milli seconds before IPOR.
Maximum device for Double Data Rate Memory Controller.
ECC sign verify SHA3_384 KAT mask.
ECC sign generation SHA3_384 KAT mask.

memberdef.name: XPLMI_ROM_KAT_MASK
memberdef.initializer: (XPLMI_SECURE_SHA3_KAT_MASK | XPLMI_SECURE_RSA_KAT_MASK |\ XPLMI_SECURE_ECC_SIGN_VERIFY_SHA3_384_KAT_MASK | XPLMI_SECURE_AES_DEC_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_SECURE_AES_CMKAT_MASK | XPLMI_SECURE_TRNG_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_SECURE_SHA384_KAT_MASK | XPLMI_SECURE_ECC_SIGN_GEN_SHA3_384_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_SHA2_256_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_SHAKE_256_KAT_MASK | XPLMI_HSS_SHA2_256_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_HSS_SHAKE_256_KAT_MASK | XPLMI_LMS_SHA2_256_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_LMS_SHAKE_256_KAT_MASK | \ XPLMI_SECURE_AES_ENC_KAT_MASK | XPLMI_SECURE_HMAC_KAT_MASK)

briefdescription.para: UART base address data structure Id.
PSMCR status check data structure Id.
Number of error outs data structure Id.
Update IPI mask data structure Id.
Glitch check on Status (excluded for Versal_2vp).

detaileddescription.para: Function pointer type for address range validation handler

memberdef.name: XPLMI_VERIFY_ADDR_RANGE
memberdef.initializer: do { \ /* Address range validation is disabled for Versal by default */ \ (void)(SubsysId); \ (void)(StartAddr); \ (void)(Len); \ (void)(StatusVar); \ (void)(ErrorCode); \ } while(0)

briefdescription.para: This macro calls the registered address validation handler (XPm_IsMemAddressValid) to ensure memory access is within valid bounds for security-critical functions.

detaileddescription.para: This feature is conditionally compiled based on PLM_ENABLE_ADDR_RANGE_VALIDATION.

parameterdescription.para: Subsystem ID
Start address of the range to verify
Length of the address range to verify
Status variable to be updated
Error code to set on failure
Label to jump to on error

briefdescription.para: 0x2 - Error when current uart selected has invalid base address
0x3 - Error when invalid uart select argument is passed
0x4 - Error when invalid uart enable argument is passed
0x5 - Error when no uart is present to configure in run-time
0x6 - Error if the permission buffer in which the access permissions has to be stored is not registered or defined
0x7 - Error if the Api Id Upper limit is greater than maximum api count
0x8 - Error if the Api Id Lower limit is greater than Api Id upper limit
0x9 - Error if the Ipi Access permission is not set properly
0xB - Error if the module id or ipi source index is not valid during ipi validation
0xC - Error if the module associated with the command's module id is not registered.

detaileddescription.para: This is checked during ipi validation

briefdescription.para: 0xD - Error if the Api Id received is greater than the maximum supported commands in the module.

detaileddescription.para: This is checked during ipi validation

briefdescription.para: 0xE - Error if the Api Id received during IPI request doesn't have IPI access.
0x10 - Error if the Api Id received during IPI request only supports secure request
0x11 - Error if Buffer memory is not available for storing
0x12 - Error when End Address stack is empty
0x13 - Error when End Address stack doesn't have a valid top address
0x14 - Error when max limit of nested begin is reached
0x15 - Error when the stack top is not valid while pushing into it
0x16 - Error when End address stack is full
0x17 - Error when End command doesn't have a valid begin
0x18 - Error when invalid ProcID is received.
0x19 - The Proc command cannot be stored or executed because the LPD is not initialized.
0x1B - Received proc does not fit in proc memory
0x1C - Error when invalid log level is received in Logging command.
0x1D - Error when invalid log buffer address is received in Logging command.
0x1E - Error when invalid log buffer length is received in Logging command.
0x23 - Error on invalid Node ID parameter.
0x24 - LPD MIO is used for WDT but LPD is not initialized.
0x25 - Invalid tamper response received for TamperTrigger IPI call
0x26 - Error when invalid log buffer type is received in Logging command.

detaileddescription.para: 0x27 - Error when registering the address range handler

briefdescription.para: 0x28 - Error when invalid address range is detected.
0x200 - Error received from SSIT Slave SLR
0x201 - Error when SSIT master times out waiting for slaves sync point

detaileddescription.para: 0x202 - Minor error if DMA initialization returns NULL during secure plm to plm communication

briefdescription.para: 0x203 - Minor error if AES initialization fails during secure plm to plm communication
0x204 - Minor error if encryption/decryption initialization fails during secure plm to plm communication
0x205 - Minor error if AAD updation fails during encryption/decryption operation
0x206 - Minor error if data updation fails during encryption/decryption operation
0x207 - Minor error if tag generation fails during encryption/decryption operation
0x200 - Invalid/No DDR region reserved for PLM.

detaileddescription.para: This is checked before the update

briefdescription.para: 0x201 - Invalid/No DDR region reserved for PLM during restore operation after the update
0x202 - Insufficient DDR region reserved for PLM
0x203 - Error while In-Place Update from Image Store
0x204 - Error while In-Place Update with Invalid Payload Len
0x205 - Error when invalid Optional Data Length is used in the PDI used for In-Place PLM Update
0x206 - Error when a task cannot be executed as the update is in progress.
0x207 - Error in shutdown initiate of modules during InPlace PLM Update
0x208 - Error when PLM Update is disabled in ROM_RSV efuse
0x209 - Error when PLM Update task is not found
0x20A - Error when PMC tries to use WDT but not enabled in design.
0x20B - Error when PMC WDT driver initialization fails.
0x20C - Invalid payload length received for the command
0x20D - ROM error during In Place Update

memberdef.definition: typedef XStatus(* XPlmi_IsAddrRangeValid_t)(u32 SubsystemId, u64 Addr, u64 Size)
memberdef.name: XPlmi_IsAddrRangeValid_t

briefdescription.para: This function provides the Slr Type.
This function is not applicable for versal_2vp.
This function is not applicable for versal_2vp.
This function is not applicable for versal_2vp.
This function provides the Iro Frequency used in ROM.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This is not applicable for Versal_2vp.
This function sets ACK's the IPI request.

parameterdescription.para: value of Plm error
value of ROM error

briefdescription.para: This function waits for specified timeout before doing IPOR.
This function provides LpdInitialized variable pointer.
This function performs initialization of platform specific RCTA registers.

inbodydescription.para: If XPAR_PSU_PSS_REF_CLK_FREQ_HZ is present in the design, it means that the reference clock frequency is known and can be stored in RTCA during initialization. If it's not defined, store 0 to indicate unknown reference clock frequency.

briefdescription.para: This function prints ROM version using ROM digest value.
This function is used to check and wait for DMA done when sending data to SSIT Slave SLRs.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to check and wait for DMA done.

parameterdescription.para: holds the address of the destination buffer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success and error code on failure

parameterdescription.para: is the address of destination

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS on success and error code on failure

parameterdescription.para: holds the address of the destination buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function processes and provides SrcAddr and DestAddr for cfi readback.

parameterdescription.para: is the type of Slr passed in readback cmd
is the pointer to the SrcAddr variable
is the pointer to the DestAddrRead variable
is the type of Slr passed in readback cmd.
is the pointer to the SrcAddr variable.
is the pointer to the DestAddrRead variable.

briefdescription.para: This will add the GIC interrupt task handler to the TaskQueue.

parameterdescription.para: is the GIC interrupt ID of the task
is the GIC interrupt ID of the task.

briefdescription.para: This function provides pointer to g_TopLevelInterruptTable.
This function provides size of g_TopLevelInterruptTable.
This function registers and enables IPI interrupt.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success and XST_FAILURE on failure

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS on success and XST_FAILURE on failure

briefdescription.para: This function registers and enables IPI interrupt.
It sets the PMC IRO frequency.

detaileddescription.para: This functions sets the PMC IRO frequency.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on success and error code failure

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS on success
XST_FAILURE on failure

inbodydescription.para: During PLM phase, set PMC IRO frequency to 400MHz for MP,HP parts Added redundancy to make it single glitch immune
Update PPU1 MB frequency used in BSP for timing calculations
During PLM phase, set PMC IRO frequency to 400MHz for MP,HP parts if production sample and for HP parts if ES1. Added redundancy to make it single glitch immune
Update PPU1 MB frequency used in BSP for timing calculations

briefdescription.para: This functions provides the PIT1 and PIT2 reset values.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to store the PIT1 reset value.
is the pointer to store the PIT12 reset value.

briefdescription.para: This function provides Gic interrupt id.

parameterdescription.para: indicates GICP source
indicates GICPx source
indicates GICP source
indicates GICPx source
indicates GICP source.
indicates GICPx source.

briefdescription.para: This function provides IPI interrupt id.

parameterdescription.para: is the Ipi target buffer index
is the Ipi target buffer index.

briefdescription.para: This function provides LpdInitialized variable pointer.

detaileddescription.para: This function provides a pointer to the UART base address variable.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the static UartBaseAddr variable that stores the UART base address (initialized to XPLMI_INVALID_UART_BASE_ADDR)

briefdescription.para: This function returns KAT status from RCTA area.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets KAT status from RTC area.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the variable which holds kat status
is the pointer to the variable which holds kat status.

briefdescription.para: Flag to indicate XRAM is available.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets XRAM Available Flag
This function sets XRAM Available Flag.

briefdescription.para: This function masks the secure stream switch value.

parameterdescription.para: - DMA0 or DMA1
DMA0 or DMA1.

briefdescription.para: This function provides pointer to IOModule structure.
This function will return KAT status of given mask.

parameterdescription.para: contains the KAT mask

simplesect.para: TRUE If KAT ran FALSE If KAT didn't ran

parameterdescription.para: contains the KAT mask

briefdescription.para: This function checks if Inplace PLM update occurs or not.

simplesect.para: TRUE if Inplace PLM Update and FALSE otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function checks if Inplace PLM update occurs or not.

simplesect.para: TRUE if Inplace PLM Update and FALSE otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function checks if Inplace PLM update is in progress or not.

simplesect.para: TRUE if Inplace PLM Update is in progress and FALSE otherwise

briefdescription.para: This file contains declarations of versal_2vp specific APIs.

para.preformatted: Ver Who Date Changes ----- ---- -------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- 2.3 sd 10/13/25 Initial release 2.4 sk 01/18/26 Added macros for LPD Domain Init Status tvp 03/06/26 Add macro for SSS config mask for SHA2 source sd 03/13/26 Added macros for hardware interrupt registers
